title: aria suecica
intro: Aria suecica, also known as Aria serotina, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the birch family Betulaceae. The bark of the Aria suecica tree contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, similar to willow bark. Traditionally, the bark has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. In herbal preparations, the bark is often dried and used as a tea, or ground into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
